## Source Record

- Source: public docs homepage for `nbadb.w4w.dev`
- Scope captured: site positioning, public navigation, release framing, and linked reference surfaces
- Capture date: `2026-04-14`

## Why It Matters

The docs site is the public contract layer for nbadb. Even when users never inspect the codebase, this surface tells them what the warehouse contains, how to navigate it, and which public reference paths exist for schema, lineage, endpoint coverage, and operational usage.

## Key Excerpts

> "NBA warehouse docs with an actual court view: endpoint coverage, possession-level lineage, and analyst-ready tables laid out like a scouting board instead of a generic software landing page."

> "Star-schema NBA data warehouse documentation. DuckDB-first with full endpoint coverage, lineage, and schema docs."

> Visible homepage routes included `Docs`, `Playground`, `Start here`, `Schema`, `Endpoints`, `Lineage`, `Guides`, and `Data Dictionary`.

> The footer explicitly links the main public release surfaces together: `GitHub`, `Kaggle`, and `PyPI`.

## Capture Notes

- The homepage is marketing-forward, but it still provides a strong summary of the public warehouse contract and user entry points.
- This surface is useful for knowledge capture because it centralizes the navigable reference system that explains nbadb beyond raw repository contents.
- The linked browser playground is a notable release signal: it lowers friction for public evaluation without requiring a local install.
